title: Moraea viscosa
intro: Moraea viscosa, also known as the sticky moraea or sticky mountain iris, is a flowering plant species native to southern Africa, characterized by its yellow or orange flowers and sticky leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin irritations, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Moraea viscosa include flavonoids, iridoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
taxonomy: Moraea viscosa is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Iridaceae. This classification places it within the genus Moraea. The Iridaceae family is a group of plants commonly known as the iris family. Moraea viscosa is a member of the tribe Irideae, which contains several genera of irises. Its classification is part of the broader taxonomy of flowering plants.
